one_line_takeaway: A pragmatic 'lessons from the trenches' case study showing that combining RAG, a QLoRA-finetuned Llama-2 7B, and a novel entities-tree context beats plain RAG or finetuning alone for on-prem QA over a private organizational hierarchy, at the cost of more verbose answers.
key_finding: On the aggregated 37-question human-evaluated set, T-RAG answered 27/37 correctly (73.0% total = Correct + Correct-Verbose), versus RAG 21/37 (56.8%) and Finetuned-only 20/37 (54.1%); however T-RAG was more verbose (6 Correct-Verbose answers vs 1 for each baseline). The entities-tree context drove most of the gain: adding tree context roughly doubled the finetuned model's correct answers on entity questions, and helped most on complex (list/compound) questions. T-RAG was also markedly more robust to context position in the Needle-in-a-Haystack test (64.1% at mid position with k=10 vs RAG's 33.3%). The MMLU check showed finetuning did not cause catastrophic forgetting (finetuned 43% vs base 45.3% overall).
core_contribution: An engineering case study on building and deploying an on-prem LLM question-answering system over a private organizational governance manual. The paper's own contributions are: (1) the deployment case study itself; (2) Tree-RAG (T-RAG), which augments standard RAG with an entities tree encoding the organization's hierarchy, from which matched entities emit textual hierarchy-description statements added to the retrieval context; (3) a new 'Correct-Verbose' (CV) evaluation metric that flags answers that are correct but include additional correct-but-irrelevant information; and (4) a Needle-in-a-Haystack test evaluating retrieval robustness to context position. The base LLM is a Llama-2 7B chat model finetuned with QLoRA on an instruction dataset generated from the organization's document. Note: the paper's Related Work discusses knowledge graphs, triples, and GraphRAG as prior/alternative approaches and explicitly contrasts its tree against them (arguing trees better capture hierarchy); these are NOT this paper's contribution.
threat_model: N/A - the paper presents no threat model, attack, or defense. Data security appears only as a deployment motivation (confidential enterprise documents cannot be sent to proprietary LLM APIs, motivating an on-prem open-source deployment). This is context, not a security contribution.
limitations: Evaluation is small-scale and single-organization: 37 aggregated QA questions plus 39 entity questions, scored by a human expert (no inter-annotator agreement reported). The primary document is private, so most results cannot be independently reproduced; the reproducible portions rely on a public UNHCR org chart used illustratively and for a 26-question eval. The headline T-RAG total (73.0%) folds in Correct-Verbose answers, and T-RAG's higher verbosity is an acknowledged tradeoff. The entities-tree approach depends on curated hierarchy data and custom string-matching NER rules, limiting generality, and applies mainly to hierarchical/entity queries. Baselines are all internal variants (base RAG, finetuned-only); there is no comparison to knowledge-graph/GraphRAG systems the paper cites. Finetuning is also noted to be sensitive to question phrasing.

datasets_used: Private organizational governance manual (a large non-profit) parsed into 1,614 generated (question, answer) pairs, split 90% train / 10% validation
UNHCR public organizational chart used illustratively and for a 26-question eval set (13 simple + 13 complex entity questions)
Entity-question evaluation set of 39 questions (17 simple + 22 complex) used for the tree-context and Needle-in-a-Haystack tests
MMLU benchmark (5-shot) used to check for finetuning-induced forgetting
quantitative_results: Aggregated 37-question human eval (total correct = Correct + Correct-Verbose): T-RAG 27/37 (73.0%), RAG 21/37 (56.8%), Finetuned-only 20/37 (54.1%)
Verbosity (Correct-Verbose count, 'All' set): T-RAG 6, RAG 1, Finetuned 1
Tree-context entity eval - Simple (17 q): RAG 10 (58.5%), Finetuned 8 (47.1%), Finetuned+tree 17 (100%), T-RAG without tree 16 (94.1%), T-RAG 17 (100%)
Tree-context entity eval - Complex (22 q): RAG 11 (50%), Finetuned 8 (36.4%), Finetuned+tree 17 (77.3%), T-RAG without tree 10 (45.5%), T-RAG 15 (68.2%)
UNHCR eval - Simple (13 q): RAG with doc chunks 6 (46.2%) vs RAG with tree 12 (92.3%); Complex (13 q): 6 (46.2%) vs 8 (61.5%)
Needle-in-a-Haystack (39 entity questions), mid position k=10: T-RAG 25/39 (64.1%) vs RAG 13/39 (33.3%); RAG top k=10 22 (56.4%), end k=10 18 (46.2%)
MMLU 5-shot overall accuracy: base Llama-2 45.3% vs finetuned 43% (no catastrophic forgetting)
QLoRA finetuning: rank r=64, ~33.5M trainable parameters (~200x reduction vs full finetuning)
baselines_compared: RAG: base (non-finetuned) Llama-2 7B with document chunks as context (context size 10 chunks)
Finetuned: QLoRA-finetuned Llama-2 7B with no retrieval context
Ablation variants: Finetuned+tree, and T-RAG without tree context, to isolate the entities-tree contribution
